description:
A customer-facing web application has been placed into maintenance mode while an 
    emergency patch was applied.  
    Ticket #425 now instructs you, the on-call IT support technician, to bring the 
    application back online.  You have SSH access as the normal user “user”.

    Do the following (2–3 terminal commands is enough):

    1. Edit the YAML configuration file
       /home/user/services/app-service/config.yml  
       so that the single key named “maintenance” is set to the boolean value false
       (i.e. the line must read exactly:  
           maintenance: false  
       with a single space after the colon).

    2. Run the provided restart helper script located at  
       /home/user/services/app-service/restart.sh  
       to apply the change.  When executed successfully the script will generate the
       file /home/user/services/app-service/restart.log as confirmation.

    3. Create a plain-text ticket note at  
       /home/user/ticket-425.log  
       containing exactly one line (no leading/trailing spaces):  
           Ticket 425 resolved

    Output / verification rules (read carefully, automated grading relies on them):
       • The key “maintenance” must be present only once in config.yml and its value
         must be “false”.  
       • /home/user/services/app-service/restart.log must exist after you run the
         helper script.  The grading system checks only that the file exists; its
         contents are not inspected.  
       • /home/user/ticket-425.log must exist and must contain exactly the single
         line “Ticket 425 resolved” followed by a newline.

    No sudo rights are available, so all commands must succeed as the regular user.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

    1. Directory tree:
       /home/user/services/app-service/
           ├── config.yml
           └── restart.sh

    2. File: /home/user/services/app-service/config.yml  
       Contents (3 lines):
           # Application service configuration
           maintenance: true
           version: 1.4.2

    3. File: /home/user/services/app-service/restart.sh  
       Contents:
           #!/bin/bash
           # Simulated restart helper
           echo "app-service restarted" > /home/user/services/app-service/restart.log
       Permissions: 0755 (already executable).

    4. The file /home/user/services/app-service/restart.log does NOT exist initially.

    5. The file /home/user/ticket-425.log does NOT exist initially.

    Expected final state after correct agent actions:

    1. /home/user/services/app-service/config.yml  
       Contents (order of non-maintenance lines may differ, comments optional):
           # Application service configuration
           maintenance: false
           version: 1.4.2

    2. /home/user/services/app-service/restart.log must exist (any content accepted).

    3. /home/user/ticket-425.log must exist and contain exactly:
           Ticket 425 resolved
       followed by a single newline.  No additional whitespace or extra lines.

    The automated test suite will parse config.yml to ensure “maintenance: false” is
    present exactly once, verify existence of restart.log, and confirm the precise
    single-line content of ticket-425.log.
